testingRequirements: Tests must validate user-facing behavior, not implementation details
Focus on what users actually do - integration tests over unit tests
Don't mock excessively - test real behavior where possible
Coverage metrics are meaningless - user flow coverage is everything

userStories.title: CLI Plan Overview

types.name: CommandDef
FlagDef

acceptanceCriteria: All new commands are additive and do not break existing command syntax.
All new actions support JSON output and `--pretty`.
New parity actions return data equivalent in meaning to corresponding MCP actions.
Search works with FTS5 and has LIKE fallback behavior.
`ticket update` supports title, description, priority, tags, and epic fields.
`tasks` resource remains the single home for task management (no duplication in `session`).
userStories.description:
# CLI Robustness Plan v2 ## Goal Make Brain Dump CLI a complete day-to-day interface that is robust, discoverable, and safe, without breaking any existing commands. Principles: - Additive only (no removals/renames that break scripts) - Resource-first command model with a small top-level power layer - Parity-first for high-impact MCP gaps - JSON-first output with `--pretty` for humans - Strong test coverage to prevent command drift ## Current Gaps (Validated) - `project` resource missing in CLI. - `ticket update` only changes status — no way to update title, description, priority, tags, or epic from CLI. - CLI lacks parity for key MCP actions: - `ticket`: `update-criterion`, `update-attachment`, `list-by-epic`, aliasable file actions - `epic`: `reconcile-learnings`, `get-learnings` - `review`: `update-demo-step` - `session`: events (task actions already covered by `tasks` resource) - `telemetry`: `log-context` - Discoverability drift between docs and implemented commands. - No consistent command metadata source for help/docs/completions. - No dedicated CLI integration test harness. ## Phase 0: Foundation (Do First) ### 0.1 CLI context resolution utilities Create `cli/lib/context.ts`: - `resolveProjectId({ projectFlag, cwd })` - precedence: explicit `--project` -> project by cwd path -> error - `resolveTicketId({ ticketFlag })` - precedence: explicit `--ticket` only for v1 (no hidden mutable state) Rationale: keeps behavior deterministic and avoids surprise "current ticket" state. ### 0.2 Command metadata registry (MVP) Create `cli/lib/command-registry.ts` as a single flat array — minimal but machine-readable. Data structure: ```typescript interface CommandDef { resource: string; // "ticket", "project", etc. action: string; // "list", "create", etc. description: string; // one-liner for help output flags: FlagDef[]; // { name, type, required, description, enum? } examples?: string[]; // "brain-dump ticket list --pretty" aliases?: string[]; // alternate action names } interface FlagDef { name: string; // "project", "ticket", etc. (without --) type: "string" | "number" | "boolean" | "enum"; required: boolean; description: string; enum?: string[]; // valid values for enum type } ``` Approach: - Build incrementally: register new Phase 1 commands first, backfill existing commands later - Each command file exports its `CommandDef[]` alongside the `handle()` function - `command-registry.ts` collects all exports into one array - `brain-dump help` reads from the registry instead of hand-written strings - Top-level commands (init, open, status, etc.) register as `resource: "_top"` or similar This stays lightweight — no decorators, no class hierarchy, just typed data. ### 0.3 CLI test harness (after Phase 1 ships) Add `cli/__tests__/` spawn-based integration tests: - isolate XDG dirs per test via `BRAIN_DUMP_DATA_DIR` env var - run `tsx cli/brain-dump.ts` with real args via `execFile` (no shell) - assert exit codes, JSON output shape, and errors - add tests retroactively for Phase 1 commands before starting Phase 2 Rationale: ship Phase 0.1 + 0.2 + Phase 1 commands first, then lock them down with tests. Tests are most valuable after command shapes stabilize. ## Phase 1: Parity-First Additions (High Impact) ## 1) Add `project` resource New file: `cli/commands/project.ts` Commands: ```bash brain-dump project list [--pretty] brain-dump project find --path [--pretty] brain-dump project create --name --path [--color ] [--pretty] brain-dump project delete --project [--confirm] [--pretty] ``` Wiring: - add import/router/help in `cli/brain-dump.ts` Core used: - `listProjects`, `findProjectByPath`, `createProject`, `deleteProject` ## 2) Expand `ticket` resource parity Modify `cli/commands/ticket.ts` Keep existing actions and add: ```bash brain-dump ticket update --ticket [--status ] [--title ] [--description ] [--priority

] [--tags ] [--epic ] [--pretty] brain-dump ticket update-status --ticket --status [--pretty] brain-dump ticket update-criterion --ticket --criterion --criterion-status [--note ] [--pretty] brain-dump ticket update-attachment --ticket --attachment --attachment-type [--attachment-description ] [--attachment-priority ] [--linked-criteria ] [--pretty] brain-dump ticket list-by-epic --epic [--project ] [--status ] [--limit ] [--pretty] brain-dump ticket link-files --ticket --files [--pretty] brain-dump ticket get-files --file [--project ] [--pretty] ``` General ticket update (title, description, priority, tags, epic): - Requires new `updateTicket()` core function in `core/ticket.ts` - Dynamic SQL SET builder: only updates fields that are provided - Validates epic exists if `--epic` given - Returns updated ticket via `getTicket()` - Existing `ticket update` behavior (status-only) is preserved when only `--status` is passed - `update-status` is an additive alias for MCP naming consistency Core used: - **New:** `updateTicket` (create in `core/ticket.ts`, export from `core/index.ts`) - `updateTicketStatus` - `updateAcceptanceCriterion` - `updateAttachmentMetadata` - `listTicketsByEpic` - `linkFiles` - `getTicketsForFile` ## 3) Expand `epic` resource parity Modify `cli/commands/epic.ts` Add: ```bash brain-dump epic reconcile-learnings --ticket --learnings-file [--update-docs] [--pretty] brain-dump epic get-learnings --epic [--pretty] ``` Core/index updates: - export `reconcileLearnings` - export `getEpicLearnings` ## 4) Expand `review` resource parity Modify `cli/commands/review.ts` Add: ```bash brain-dump review update-demo-step --demo-script --step-order --step-status [--step-notes ] [--pretty] ``` Core used: - `updateDemoStep` ## 5) Expand `session` resource parity (events only) Modify `cli/commands/session.ts` Add event actions only (task actions already live in the `tasks` resource — no duplication): ```bash brain-dump session update-state --session --state [--message ] [--pretty] brain-dump session emit-event --session --event-type [--event-data-file ] [--pretty] brain-dump session get-events --session [--since ] [--limit ] [--pretty] brain-dump session clear-events --session [--pretty] ``` Compatibility: - keep existing `session update` unchanged - `update-state` is additive alias for MCP naming consistency Core used: - `updateState`, `emitEvent`, `getEvents`, `clearEvents` ## 6) Expand `telemetry` parity Modify `cli/commands/telemetry.ts` Add: ```bash brain-dump telemetry log-context --session --has-description --has-criteria [--criteria-count ] [--comment-count ] [--attachment-count ] [--image-count ] [--pretty] ``` Core used: - `logContext` ## Phase 2: Top-Level Power Commands (Small, High Leverage) These remain top-level additions in `cli/brain-dump.ts` (additive only). ## 7) `brain-dump open` ```bash brain-dump open [--port ] ``` Behavior: - default port `4242` - platform-aware opener: `xdg-open` (Linux), `open` (macOS), `start` (Windows) - optional health check to `http://localhost:/` before opening - if server not running, print helpful message: "Dev server not running. Start it with: pnpm dev" Implementation: ~15 lines inline in `cli/brain-dump.ts`. No core functions needed. ## 8) `brain-dump init` ```bash brain-dump init [--name ] [--color ] [--pretty] ``` Behavior: - resolve cwd - check `findProjectByPath(cwd)` - if exists: return existing project - else create project with: - name: `--name` -> `package.json:name` -> directory basename - path: cwd ## 9) `brain-dump status` ```bash brain-dump status [--project ] [--limit ] [--pretty] ``` Output sections: - project summary by ticket status - active tickets (`in_progress`, `ai_review`, `human_review`) - active Ralph sessions snapshot - recent activity/comments Implementation: - new `cli/commands/status.ts` for structured pretty output - JSON mode returns a single normalized object payload ## 10) `brain-dump search` ```bash brain-dump search "" [--project ] [--status ] [--limit ] [--pretty] ``` Implementation: - new `core/search.ts` with `searchTickets()` - FTS5-first query, LIKE fallback if unavailable - expose from `core/index.ts` - add DB setup in `core/db.ts` for `tickets_fts` + triggers (port from `src/lib/db.ts`) ## 11) `brain-dump context` ```bash brain-dump context --ticket [--pretty] ``` Read-only ticket deep dive payload: - ticket (full detail from `getTicket()`) - comments (from `listComments()`) - review findings (from `getFindings()`) - subtasks/criteria (inline in ticket data) ## 12) `brain-dump log` ```bash brain-dump log [--project ] [--ticket ] [--limit ] [--pretty] ``` Implementation: - new `getActivityLog()` in `core/comment.ts` - joined ticket/project comment stream ordered desc ## Phase 3: UX and Maintenance Hardening ## 13) `brain-dump completions` ```bash brain-dump completions zsh brain-dump completions bash brain-dump completions fish ``` Generate scripts from `command-registry.ts`. ## 14) Help and unknown-command UX - Add "did you mean" suggestion for unknown resource/action. - Ensure all help text comes from command registry metadata. ## 15) Docs sync and drift prevention - Add a script to generate `docs/cli.md` command tables from registry metadata. - Align/remove stale references to unsupported commands in docs. ## File-Level Change Plan Create: - `cli/commands/project.ts` — Phase 1, #1 - `cli/commands/status.ts` — Phase 2, #9 - `cli/lib/context.ts` — Phase 0.1 - `cli/lib/command-registry.ts` — Phase 0.2 - `core/search.ts` — Phase 2, #10 - `core/ticket.ts` → add `updateTicket()` — Phase 1, #2 - `cli/__tests__/...` — Phase 0.3 (after Phase 1 ships) Modify: - `cli/brain-dump.ts` — all phases (routing, help, top-level commands) - `cli/commands/ticket.ts` — Phase 1, #2 - `cli/commands/epic.ts` — Phase 1, #3 - `cli/commands/review.ts` — Phase 1, #4 - `cli/commands/session.ts` — Phase 1, #5 - `cli/commands/telemetry.ts` — Phase 1, #6 - `core/index.ts` — export new functions - `core/comment.ts` — Phase 2, #12 (`getActivityLog()`) - `core/db.ts` — Phase 2, #10 (FTS5 init) - `docs/cli.md` — Phase 3, #15 (auto-generated) ## Acceptance Criteria - All new commands are additive and do not break existing command syntax. - All new actions support JSON output and `--pretty`. - New parity actions return data equivalent in meaning to corresponding MCP actions. - `brain-dump help` lists all new resources/actions. - Unknown commands provide suggested alternatives (Phase 3). - Search works with FTS5 and has LIKE fallback behavior. - `ticket update` supports title, description, priority, tags, and epic fields. - `tasks` resource remains the single home for task management (no duplication in `session`). - CLI tests cover (Phase 0.3, after Phase 1): - success paths - validation failures - dry-run destructive flows - JSON shape consistency ## Verification Plan Per milestone: 1. `pnpm type-check` 2. `pnpm lint` 3. `pnpm test` 4. CLI integration test suite pass (from Phase 0.3 onward) Manual smoke: ```bash brain-dump open brain-dump init --name "test-project" brain-dump project list --pretty brain-dump ticket list --project --pretty brain-dump ticket update --ticket --title "New title" --priority high --pretty brain-dump status --pretty brain-dump search "workflow" --pretty brain-dump log --limit 10 --pretty brain-dump context --ticket --pretty brain-dump session emit-event --session --event-type progress --event-data-file ./event.json --pretty brain-dump telemetry log-context --session --has-description --has-criteria --criteria-count 3 --pretty ``` ## Explicit Non-Goals (This Iteration) - Removing existing commands or changing semantics in a breaking way - Introducing hidden mutable "current ticket" state - Re-architecting MCP tools to match CLI; CLI should consume existing core APIs - Expanding into destructive DB maintenance commands beyond current safe patterns - Duplicating `tasks` resource actions into `session` resource

userStories.title: Phase 0.1: CLI context resolution utilities
userStories.overview:
Create `cli/lib/context.ts` with shared context resolution utilities used by all CLI commands.
**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.
acceptanceCriteria: `resolveProjectId` resolves from `--project` flag first, then cwd, then errors
Both functions have proper TypeScript types and error messages
userStories.description:
## Overview

Create `cli/lib/context.ts` with shared context resolution utilities used by all CLI commands.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## What to Build

### `resolveProjectId({ projectFlag, cwd })`
- Precedence: explicit `--project` flag → project by cwd path (via `findProjectByPath`) → throw error
- Used by any command that needs a project context

### `resolveTicketId({ ticketFlag })`
- Precedence: explicit `--ticket` flag only for v1 (no hidden mutable state)
- Deterministic behavior — no "current ticket" concept

## Files

- **Create:** `cli/lib/context.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `resolveProjectId` resolves from `--project` flag first, then cwd, then errors
- [ ] `resolveTicketId` requires explicit `--ticket` flag (no implicit state)
- [ ] Both functions have proper TypeScript types and error messages
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes

userStories.title: Phase 0.2: Command metadata registry
userStories.overview:
Create `cli/lib/command-registry.ts` — a single flat array of `CommandDef` objects that serves as the machine-readable source of truth for all CLI commands. Used for help output, completions, and docs generation.
**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

types.name: CommandDef
FlagDef

acceptanceCriteria: Registry collects commands from all command files
`brain-dump help` reads from registry instead of hand-written strings
userStories.description:
## Overview

Create `cli/lib/command-registry.ts` — a single flat array of `CommandDef` objects that serves as the machine-readable source of truth for all CLI commands. Used for help output, completions, and docs generation.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## What to Build

### Interfaces
```typescript
interface CommandDef {
  resource: string;          // "ticket", "project", etc.
  action: string;            // "list", "create", etc.
  description: string;       // one-liner for help output
  flags: FlagDef[];          // { name, type, required, description, enum? }
  examples?: string[];       // "brain-dump ticket list --pretty"
  aliases?: string[];        // alternate action names
}

interface FlagDef {
  name: string;
  type: "string" | "number" | "boolean" | "enum";
  required: boolean;
  description: string;
  enum?: string[];
}
```

### Approach
- Build incrementally: register Phase 1 commands first, backfill existing later
- Each command file exports its `CommandDef[]` alongside the `handle()` function
- `command-registry.ts` collects all exports into one array
- Top-level commands register as `resource: "_top"`
- No decorators, no class hierarchy — just typed data

## Files

- **Create:** `cli/lib/command-registry.ts`
- **Modify:** `cli/brain-dump.ts` (wire `help` to read from registry)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `CommandDef` and `FlagDef` interfaces exported
- [ ] Registry collects commands from all command files
- [ ] `brain-dump help` reads from registry instead of hand-written strings
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes

## Overview

Create `cli/commands/project.ts` to add the missing `project` resource to the CLI. Currently only available via MCP.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## Commands to Implement

```bash
brain-dump project list [--pretty]
brain-dump project find --path <path> [--pretty]
brain-dump project create --name <name> --path <path> [--color <hex>] [--pretty]
brain-dump project delete --project <id> [--confirm] [--pretty]
```

## Core Functions Used

- `listProjects`
- `findProjectByPath`
- `createProject`
- `deleteProject`

## Files

- **Create:** `cli/commands/project.ts`
- **Modify:** `cli/brain-dump.ts` (add import, routing, help entry)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 4 project actions work (list, find, create, delete)
- [ ] JSON output by default, `--pretty` for human-readable
- [ ] `delete` requires `--confirm` flag (dry-run by default)
- [ ] Registered in command registry
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` and `pnpm lint` pass

userStories.title: Phase 1.2: Expand `ticket` CLI resource parity
userStories.overview:
Expand `cli/commands/ticket.ts` with missing MCP-parity actions and add a general `updateTicket()` core function.
**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.
acceptanceCriteria: `ticket update` supports title, description, priority, tags, and epic fields
`updateTicket()` core function uses dynamic SQL SET builder
userStories.description:
## Overview Expand `cli/commands/ticket.ts` with missing MCP-parity actions and add a general `updateTicket()` core function. **Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan. ## New Commands ```bash brain-dump ticket update --ticket [--status ] [--title ] [--description ] [--priority

] [--tags ] [--epic ] [--pretty] brain-dump ticket update-status --ticket --status [--pretty] brain-dump ticket update-criterion --ticket --criterion --criterion-status [--note ] [--pretty] brain-dump ticket update-attachment --ticket --attachment --attachment-type [--attachment-description ] [--attachment-priority ] [--linked-criteria ] [--pretty] brain-dump ticket list-by-epic --epic [--project ] [--status ] [--limit ] [--pretty] brain-dump ticket link-files --ticket --files [--pretty] brain-dump ticket get-files --file [--project ] [--pretty] ``` ## Core Changes - **New:** `updateTicket()` in `core/ticket.ts` — dynamic SQL SET builder, only updates provided fields, validates epic exists if given, returns updated ticket via `getTicket()` - **Export** from `core/index.ts` ## Existing Core Used - `updateTicketStatus`, `updateAcceptanceCriterion`, `updateAttachmentMetadata` - `listTicketsByEpic`, `linkFiles`, `getTicketsForFile` ## Key Behaviors - Existing `ticket update` (status-only) preserved when only `--status` passed - `update-status` is an additive alias for MCP naming consistency - Tags passed as comma-separated string, parsed to array ## Files - **Create/modify:** `core/ticket.ts` (add `updateTicket()`) - **Modify:** `cli/commands/ticket.ts`, `core/index.ts` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 7 new ticket actions work - [ ] `ticket update` supports title, description, priority, tags, and epic fields - [ ] Existing `ticket update --status` behavior unchanged - [ ] `updateTicket()` core function uses dynamic SQL SET builder - [ ] JSON + `--pretty` output for all actions - [ ] `pnpm type-check` and `pnpm lint` pass

userStories.title: Phase 1.3: Expand `epic` CLI resource parity
userStories.overview:
Add missing `reconcile-learnings` and `get-learnings` actions to `cli/commands/epic.ts`.
**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.
acceptanceCriteria: `--learnings-file` reads and parses JSON array from file path
userStories.description:
## Overview

Add missing `reconcile-learnings` and `get-learnings` actions to `cli/commands/epic.ts`.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## New Commands

```bash
brain-dump epic reconcile-learnings --ticket <id> --learnings-file <path> [--update-docs] [--pretty]
brain-dump epic get-learnings --epic <id> [--pretty]
```

## Details

- `reconcile-learnings` reads a JSON file of learning objects from `--learnings-file`
- `get-learnings` returns accumulated learnings for an epic across completed tickets

## Core Functions Used

- `reconcileLearnings`
- `getEpicLearnings`

## Files

- **Modify:** `cli/commands/epic.ts`, `core/index.ts` (ensure exports)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Both new epic actions work
- [ ] `--learnings-file` reads and parses JSON array from file path
- [ ] JSON + `--pretty` output
- [ ] Registered in command registry
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` and `pnpm lint` pass

## Overview

Add event-related actions to `cli/commands/session.ts`. Task actions already live in `tasks` resource — no duplication.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## New Commands

```bash
brain-dump session update-state --session <id> --state <state> [--message <text>] [--pretty]
brain-dump session emit-event --session <id> --event-type <thinking|tool_start|tool_end|file_change|progress|state_change|error> [--event-data-file <path>] [--pretty]
brain-dump session get-events --session <id> [--since <iso>] [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
brain-dump session clear-events --session <id> [--pretty]
```

## Compatibility

- Keep existing `session update` unchanged
- `update-state` is an additive alias for MCP naming consistency

## Core Functions Used

- `updateState`, `emitEvent`, `getEvents`, `clearEvents`

- [ ] `--event-type` validates against enum
- [ ] Existing `session update` behavior unchanged

userStories.title: Phase 2.7: `brain-dump open` command
userStories.overview:
Add `brain-dump open` top-level command to open the Brain Dump UI in the browser.
**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.
acceptanceCriteria: Helpful error message when server not running
userStories.description:
## Overview

Add `brain-dump open` top-level command to open the Brain Dump UI in the browser.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## Command

```bash
brain-dump open [--port <n>]
```

## Behavior

- Default port `4242`
- Platform-aware opener: `xdg-open` (Linux), `open` (macOS), `start` (Windows)
- Optional health check to `http://localhost:<port>/` before opening
- If server not running, print helpful message: "Dev server not running. Start it with: pnpm dev"

## Implementation

~15 lines inline in `cli/brain-dump.ts`. No core functions needed.

## Files

- **Modify:** `cli/brain-dump.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `brain-dump open` opens browser to localhost:4242
- [ ] `--port` flag overrides default port
- [ ] Helpful error message when server not running
- [ ] Platform detection works for Linux/macOS/Windows
- [ ] Registered in command registry

userStories.title: Phase 2.8: `brain-dump init` command
userStories.overview:
Add `brain-dump init` top-level command to register the current directory as a Brain Dump project.
**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.
acceptanceCriteria: `brain-dump init` creates a project for current directory
Idempotent — returns existing project if already registered
Name resolution: flag → package.json → directory basename
userStories.description:
## Overview

Add `brain-dump init` top-level command to register the current directory as a Brain Dump project.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## Command

```bash
brain-dump init [--name <name>] [--color <hex>] [--pretty]
```

## Behavior

- Resolve cwd
- Check `findProjectByPath(cwd)`
- If exists: return existing project (idempotent)
- Else create project with:
  - name: `--name` → `package.json:name` → directory basename
  - path: cwd

## Files

- **Modify:** `cli/brain-dump.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `brain-dump init` creates a project for current directory
- [ ] Idempotent — returns existing project if already registered
- [ ] Name resolution: flag → package.json → directory basename
- [ ] JSON + `--pretty` output
- [ ] Registered in command registry

userStories.title: Phase 2.9: `brain-dump status` command
userStories.overview:
Add `brain-dump status` top-level command for a project dashboard view.
**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.
acceptanceCriteria: `--project` flag selects project (or resolves from cwd)
Pretty output has clear sections with headers
JSON output is a single normalized object
userStories.description:
## Overview

Add `brain-dump status` top-level command for a project dashboard view.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## Command

```bash
brain-dump status [--project <id>] [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
```

## Output Sections

- Project summary by ticket status (counts per status)
- Active tickets (`in_progress`, `ai_review`, `human_review`)
- Active Ralph sessions snapshot
- Recent activity/comments

## Implementation

- New `cli/commands/status.ts` for structured pretty output
- JSON mode returns a single normalized object payload

## Files

- **Create:** `cli/commands/status.ts`
- **Modify:** `cli/brain-dump.ts` (routing)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `brain-dump status` shows project dashboard
- [ ] `--project` flag selects project (or resolves from cwd)
- [ ] Pretty output has clear sections with headers
- [ ] JSON output is a single normalized object
- [ ] Registered in command registry
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` and `pnpm lint` pass

userStories.title: Phase 2.10: `brain-dump search` command
userStories.overview:
Add `brain-dump search` command with FTS5 full-text search and LIKE fallback.
**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.
acceptanceCriteria: FTS5 used when available, LIKE fallback otherwise
userStories.description:
## Overview

Add `brain-dump search` command with FTS5 full-text search and LIKE fallback.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## Command

```bash
brain-dump search "<query>" [--project <id>] [--status <status>] [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
```

## Implementation

- New `core/search.ts` with `searchTickets()` function
- FTS5-first query, LIKE fallback if FTS5 unavailable
- Expose from `core/index.ts`
- Add DB setup in `core/db.ts` for `tickets_fts` virtual table + triggers (port from `src/lib/db.ts`)

## Files

- **Create:** `core/search.ts`
- **Modify:** `core/db.ts` (FTS5 init), `core/index.ts` (export), `cli/brain-dump.ts` (routing)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `brain-dump search "query"` returns matching tickets
- [ ] FTS5 used when available, LIKE fallback otherwise
- [ ] `--project`, `--status`, `--limit` filters work
- [ ] JSON + `--pretty` output
- [ ] `tickets_fts` table and triggers created in `core/db.ts`
- [ ] Registered in command registry
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` and `pnpm lint` pass

userStories.title: Phase 2.11: `brain-dump context` command

Add `brain-dump context` command for a read-only deep dive into a ticket's full context.

acceptanceCriteria: Includes ticket details, comments, findings, and criteria
userStories.description:
## Overview

Add `brain-dump context` command for a read-only deep dive into a ticket's full context.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## Command

```bash
brain-dump context --ticket <id> [--pretty]
```

## Output Payload

- Ticket full detail (from `getTicket()`)
- Comments (from `listComments()`)
- Review findings (from `getFindings()`)
- Subtasks/criteria (inline in ticket data)

## Files

- **Modify:** `cli/brain-dump.ts` (routing + inline handler or new command file)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `brain-dump context --ticket <id>` returns combined ticket context
- [ ] Includes ticket details, comments, findings, and criteria
- [ ] JSON + `--pretty` output
- [ ] Registered in command registry
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` and `pnpm lint` pass

Add `brain-dump log` command for a chronological activity stream across tickets.

acceptanceCriteria: `brain-dump log` shows recent activity across project
userStories.description:
## Overview

Add `brain-dump log` command for a chronological activity stream across tickets.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## Command

```bash
brain-dump log [--project <id>] [--ticket <id>] [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
```

## Implementation

- New `getActivityLog()` function in `core/comment.ts`
- Joined ticket/project comment stream ordered descending by date
- Export from `core/index.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--project` and `--ticket` filters work

- [ ] JSON + `--pretty` output

userStories.description:
## Overview

Add `brain-dump completions` command to generate shell completion scripts from the command registry.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## Commands

```bash
brain-dump completions zsh
brain-dump completions bash
brain-dump completions fish
```

## Implementation

- Generate completion scripts from `command-registry.ts` data
- Each shell gets its own output format
- Users pipe output to their shell config (e.g., `brain-dump completions zsh >> ~/.zshrc`)

## Files

- **Modify:** `cli/brain-dump.ts` (routing + completion generator)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `brain-dump completions zsh` outputs valid zsh completion script
- [ ] `brain-dump completions bash` outputs valid bash completion script
- [ ] `brain-dump completions fish` outputs valid fish completion script
- [ ] Scripts generated from command registry (not hand-written)
- [ ] Registered in command registry

Improve help output and add "did you mean?" suggestions for unknown commands.

userStories.description:
## Overview

Improve help output and add "did you mean?" suggestions for unknown commands.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## What to Build

- Add "did you mean" suggestion for unknown resource/action (e.g., `brain-dump tickt list` → "Did you mean: ticket?")
- Ensure all help text comes from command registry metadata (no hand-written strings)
- Levenshtein distance or similar for fuzzy matching

## Files

- **Modify:** `cli/brain-dump.ts` (unknown command handler, help output)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Unknown commands show "did you mean" suggestion with closest match
- [ ] All help text generated from command registry
- [ ] `brain-dump help` shows all resources and their actions
- [ ] `brain-dump help <resource>` shows actions for that resource
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` and `pnpm lint` pass

userStories.title: Phase 3.15: Docs sync and drift prevention

Add automated docs generation from the command registry to prevent documentation drift.

acceptanceCriteria: Script generates `docs/cli.md` from command registry data
Generated docs include all resources, actions, flags, and examples
Stale/incorrect command references removed from docs
Script can be run as part of CI or pre-commit
userStories.description:
## Overview

Add automated docs generation from the command registry to prevent documentation drift.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## What to Build

- Script to generate `docs/cli.md` command tables from registry metadata
- Align/remove stale references to unsupported commands in existing docs
- Could be a `brain-dump docs generate` command or standalone script

## Files

- **Create/modify:** Script or command for docs generation
- **Modify:** `docs/cli.md` (auto-generated output)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Script generates `docs/cli.md` from command registry data
- [ ] Generated docs include all resources, actions, flags, and examples
- [ ] Stale/incorrect command references removed from docs
- [ ] Script can be run as part of CI or pre-commit
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` and `pnpm lint` pass

userStories.title: Phase 0.3: CLI integration test harness
userStories.overview:
Add spawn-based integration tests for CLI commands. Intentionally done after Phase 1 ships so command shapes are stable before locking them down.
**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.
acceptanceCriteria: Test harness isolates data directory per test
All Phase 1 commands have success-path tests
Validation failure tests for required flags
`pnpm test` includes and passes CLI tests
userStories.description:
## Overview

Add spawn-based integration tests for CLI commands. Intentionally done after Phase 1 ships so command shapes are stable before locking them down.

**Reference:** See "CLI Plan Overview" ticket (d09247c6) for the full plan.

## What to Build

### Test Infrastructure (`cli/__tests__/`)
- Isolate XDG dirs per test via `BRAIN_DUMP_DATA_DIR` env var
- Run `tsx cli/brain-dump.ts` with real args via `execFile` (no shell)
- Assert exit codes, JSON output shape, and errors

### Test Coverage
- Success paths for all Phase 1 commands
- Validation failures (missing required flags, invalid values)
- Dry-run destructive flows (`project delete` without `--confirm`)
- JSON shape consistency (output parses as valid JSON)

## Files

- **Create:** `cli/__tests__/` directory with test files

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test harness isolates data directory per test
- [ ] Tests use `execFile` (no shell injection risk)
- [ ] All Phase 1 commands have success-path tests
- [ ] Validation failure tests for required flags
- [ ] Dry-run tests for destructive actions
- [ ] JSON output shape assertions
- [ ] `pnpm test` includes and passes CLI tests

techStack: Database: SQLite with better-sqlite3, Drizzle ORM
State: TanStack Query for server state

dos: Use Drizzle ORM: `db.select().from(tickets)`
Use typed schema imports: `import { tickets } from "../lib/schema"`
Use `eq()`, `and()`, `sql` from drizzle-orm for conditions
Use transactions for multi-table operations: `db.transaction(() => {...})`
Use `.get()` for single row, `.all()` for multiple
donts: Raw SQL strings: `db.run("SELECT * FROM tickets")`
String-based table names
String concatenation for WHERE clauses
Multiple independent queries that should be atomic
Assume query returns what you expect without checking

dos: Use `useQuery` with `queryKeys` for data fetching
Use `useMutation` with `onSuccess` for state changes
Invalidate queries after mutations: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tickets })`
Use centralized `queryKeys` object from `src/lib/hooks.ts`
Create custom hooks in `src/lib/hooks.ts` for reusable query logic
donts: `useState` + `useEffect` for fetched data
Direct API calls without proper cache invalidation
Manual cache manipulation or refetch after every change
Hardcoded query key strings throughout components
Duplicate query setup in multiple components

dos: Use Tailwind classes for static styles: `className="p-4 bg-slate-800"`
Use CSS variables for theming: `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`
Reserve inline styles for dynamic/computed values: `style={{ width: `${percent}%` }}`
Define shared style constants at module level for referential stability
Use `React.CSSProperties` type for inline style objects when needed
donts: Inline style objects for single-use styles
Hardcoded colors that don't adapt to themes
Mix Tailwind and inline styles inconsistently
Create new objects inside components that are reused
Untyped style objects that miss IDE autocomplete

dos: Use `createServerFn({ method: "GET" })` for reads, `"POST"` for writes
Use `.inputValidator()` for type-safe input handling
Return typed data directly: `return db.select().from(tickets).all()`
Use `ensureExists()` for required lookups: `ensureExists(project, "Project")`
Import from `@tanstack/react-start` (not `@tanstack/react-start/server`)
donts: Mix GET/POST inconsistently
Access raw input without validation
Wrap in unnecessary response objects
Return null and let caller handle missing data
Wrong import path

dos: Use Zod schemas for input validation: `{ ticketId: z.string() }`
Return structured `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: ... }] }` format
Set `isError: true` for error responses
Use `log.info()` / `log.error()` from `../lib/logging.js`
Include helpful error messages: `"Project not found. Use list_projects to see available."`
donts: Trust input without validation
Return plain strings
Return error text without the error flag
`console.log` in MCP server code
Generic "Not found" errors

dos: Test user behavior: what users see, click, and experience
Integration tests for workflows: test components together
Real database fixtures with actual schema
Tests that fail when user-facing behavior breaks
Ask: "Does this test catch bugs users would encounter?"
donts: Test implementation details, internal state, or private methods
Unit tests for every function
Excessive mocking of internals
Tests that break on refactoring internals
Chase 100% code coverage as a goal

verificationSteps: Run `pnpm type-check` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm lint` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm test` - all tests must pass
Added tests for new functionality (ONLY tests that verify real user behavior - see Testing Philosophy above)
Used typed error classes (not generic `Error`)
Used Drizzle ORM (not raw SQL) - see DO/DON'T table above
Followed existing patterns from DO/DON'T tables
No hardcoded values that should be configurable
Existing tests still pass
No regressions in related functionality
Updated tests if behavior changed
Did not break backward compatibility (unless explicitly requested)
Manually verified in browser at `localhost:4242`
Checked responsive layout
Verified TanStack Query invalidates and updates correctly
Accessibility: keyboard navigation works, proper ARIA labels
Migration file created via `pnpm db:generate`
Migration tested with `pnpm db:migrate`
Backup tested if schema changed (use `pnpm brain-dump backup` then test restore)
Updated `src/lib/schema.ts` with proper types and constraints
Tested tool via Claude Code integration
Verified error responses are informative (see DO/DON'T table)
Updated tool documentation if interface changed
Added Zod schema for input validation
All acceptance criteria from ticket met
Work summary added via `comment` tool, `action: "add"` (for Ralph sessions)
Session completed with appropriate outcome (for Ralph sessions)
Committed with proper message format: `feat(<ticket-id>): <description>`

epicDescription: Make Brain Dump CLI a complete day-to-day interface that is robust, discoverable, and safe. Covers foundation work (context resolution, command registry, test harness), MCP parity additions (project, ticket, epic, review, session, telemetry), top-level power commands (open, init, status, search, context, log), and UX hardening (completions, help, docs sync). All work lives on one shared branch.
